page: Google Cloud Platform ct5p-hightpu-2t is an accelerator-optimized server instance featuring 104 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 2.15 GB per vCPU. Acceleration is driven by two integrated Google TPU ct5p chips.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage or cloud storage services. Additionally, the instance includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external networking configurations for public internet access.

Given the high density of dedicated vCPUs and specialized TPU hardware, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads that leverage hardware accelerators. The low memory-per-core ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive database tasks, but highly efficient for machine learning training, deep learning inference, and large-scale parallel computing workloads that offload processing to the dual TPU accelerators.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct5p-hightpu-2t is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 224.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.15 GB of RAM per core. The primary processing capability is enhanced by two integrated Google TPU ct5p hardware accelerators. This instance does not feature any local storage, necessitating network-attached storage solutions for data persistence, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Due to the high vCPU count and dual TPU configuration, this server is designed for parallel computing tasks, machine learning model training, and neural network inference workloads that can offload execution to specialized TPU hardware, while the low memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform ct5p-hightpu-2t features 104 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 224.0 GB RAM, and two Google TPU ct5p accelerators, designed for machine learning and parallel computing workloads.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring one hundred and four dedicated vCPUs and dual integrated Tensor Processing Units for parallel computing workloads.

No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage
